evidence_excerpt: After transient benefit from balloon dilatation, he underwent robotic-assisted Heller’s myotomy with marked improvement and weight gain; follow-up barium swallow showed normal emptying and no reflux. He remains on hydrocortisone for adrenal insufficiency (Allgrove’s syndrome).

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Reported 'marked improvement' in symptoms and normal esophageal emptying without reflux after surgery; no persistent severe symptoms documented.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Esophageal balloon dilatation with only transient improvement.
Subsequently, he underwent Robotic-assisted Heller’s myotomy using the DaVinci system.
Postoperatively there was marked improvement with an increase in his weight.
Follow-up barium swallow showed normal emptying of the esophagus with no evidence of gastroesophageal reflux.

notes_on_uncertainty: The source is a multi-patient case report. This summary focuses on the 2.5-year-old male with Allgrove’s syndrome due to the clearest follow-up details. Exact intervals from diagnosis to interventions and follow-up are not provided; short-term and mid-term periods are inferred. The overall outcome is classified as partial recovery because of ongoing adrenal insufficiency management despite apparent resolution of achalasia-related obstruction.
